title: MITRE ATT&CK Cloud -- Technique to Defensive Controls Mapping
description: Maps MITRE ATT&CK Cloud techniques to CSA CCM v4 controls, defensive measures, and detection methods. Use to prioritise cloud security controls based on threat actor TTPs or to understand which controls address specific cloud attack scenarios.
verificationNote: MITRE ATT&CK Cloud technique IDs are public domain from attack.mitre.org. Control mappings are reasoned assessments -- not normatively specified by MITRE or CSA.

mappings.description: Adversary obtains and uses valid cloud account credentials (compromised, leaked, or purchased) to gain access to cloud services. Most common initial access vector in cloud environments.
defensiveControls: Enforce phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2/WebAuthn) on all accounts
Implement conditional access policies (device compliance, location, risk score)
Deploy credential exposure monitoring (darkweb, paste sites)
Enforce password complexity and rotation policies
Implement impossible travel detection
detectionMethods: Monitor for anomalous login patterns (new location, device, time)
Alert on impossible travel (login from geographically distant locations within short timeframe)
Detect credential stuffing through authentication failure rate analysis
Monitor for logins from known malicious IPs (threat intelligence integration)

mappings.description: Adversary specifically targets cloud service accounts (AWS IAM, Azure AD, GCP IAM) through phishing, credential theft, or API key exposure.
defensiveControls: Eliminate long-lived API keys in favour of temporary credentials (STS, Managed Identity)
Implement just-in-time privileged access
Pre-commit secret scanning for API keys
Monitor for API key usage from unexpected sources
detectionMethods: Alert on API key usage from new IP addresses or regions
Monitor for service account credential usage outside normal patterns
Detect API key creation for existing service accounts

mappings.description: Adversary exploits vulnerabilities in internet-facing cloud applications, APIs, or services to gain initial access. Includes web application vulnerabilities, API injection, and cloud service misconfigurations.
defensiveControls: Deploy WAF with OWASP Top 10 rule sets
Implement API gateway with request validation
Conduct regular vulnerability scanning and penetration testing
Apply timely patching for cloud-hosted applications
Implement rate limiting and bot detection
detectionMethods: WAF alert correlation for attack patterns
Application-layer anomaly detection
Monitor for unexpected outbound connections from web servers
API call pattern analysis for injection attempts

mappings.description: Adversary compromises a trusted third party (SaaS vendor, CI/CD service, managed service provider) that has access to the target's cloud environment through cross-account roles or API integrations.
defensiveControls: Minimize cross-account trust relationships
Implement external ID conditions on cross-account roles
Regularly audit third-party access permissions
Require MFA for third-party access
Monitor third-party security posture
detectionMethods: Monitor cross-account role assumption events
Alert on new cross-account trust relationships
Detect unusual API calls from third-party principals
Monitor for third-party vendor security advisories

mappings.description: Adversary accesses data stored in cloud storage services (S3, Blob Storage, GCS) through misconfigured permissions, stolen credentials, or exploited access policies. The Capital One breach (2019) is the canonical example.
defensiveControls: Enable account-level block public access on all storage
Implement CMEK encryption for sensitive data
Deploy CSPM to detect public storage continuously
Enforce least-privilege storage access policies
Enable server access logging on all buckets
detectionMethods: Alert on storage policy changes (especially public access)
Monitor for bulk download patterns from storage services
Detect unusual cross-account storage access
Monitor server access logs for anomalous source IPs

mappings.description: Adversary exfiltrates data by transferring it to an attacker-controlled cloud account. Difficult to detect because the traffic appears as normal cloud-to-cloud data transfer.
defensiveControls: Implement VPC endpoints to restrict cloud API calls to specific VPCs
Block cross-account data transfer through SCPs/policies
Implement DLP for cloud storage and egress
Restrict S3/Blob copy operations to known accounts
detectionMethods: Monitor CloudTrail/audit logs for cross-account copy operations
Alert on storage replication to unknown accounts
Detect data transfer volume anomalies
Monitor for snapshot sharing to external accounts

mappings.description: Adversary deploys resources in cloud regions where the organisation has no monitoring or security controls. Unused regions are blind spots for detection.
defensiveControls: Restrict resource creation to approved regions via SCPs/policies
Enable CloudTrail/audit logging in ALL regions
Deploy CSPM with full-region coverage
Alert on any activity in non-approved regions
detectionMethods: Alert on any API call or resource creation in non-approved regions
Monitor for IAM role creation in unused regions
CSPM scan for resources in all regions

mappings.description: Adversary modifies cloud compute resources (create snapshot, create instance from snapshot, revert instance) to evade security controls or establish persistence.
defensiveControls: Restrict compute modification permissions to authorized roles
Implement tagging requirements for all compute resources
Deploy IaC drift detection
Monitor for unauthorized snapshot creation/sharing
detectionMethods: Alert on snapshot creation, sharing, or copying to external accounts
Monitor for instance launches from non-approved AMIs/images
Detect compute instances created outside IaC pipeline
Alert on security group modifications on compute instances

mappings.techniqueName: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
mappings.description: Adversary exploits SSRF or application vulnerability to access cloud instance metadata API (169.254.169.254) and retrieve temporary credentials. This was the attack vector in the Capital One breach (2019).
defensiveControls: Enable IMDSv2 (Instance Metadata Service v2) on all EC2 instances
Implement network-level SSRF protection
Restrict metadata API access through iptables/network policy
Use IAM roles with minimal permissions on compute instances
detectionMethods: Monitor for metadata API calls from application processes
Alert on credential usage from unexpected source IPs
Detect SSRF patterns in application WAF logs
Monitor for metadata API access attempts blocked by IMDSv2

mappings.description: Adversary modifies cloud account properties (add credentials, modify permissions, create backdoor users) to maintain persistent access. Includes adding API keys to existing users, modifying IAM policies, or creating new privileged accounts.
defensiveControls: Enforce MFA for all IAM changes
Implement SCPs preventing IAM modification outside approved pipelines
Deploy real-time alerting on IAM changes
Conduct daily IAM drift detection
detectionMethods: Alert on new IAM user/role creation
Detect API key creation for existing users
Monitor IAM policy modifications (especially privilege escalation patterns)
Alert on MFA device changes for privileged accounts

mappings.description: Adversary enumerates cloud infrastructure (instances, storage, databases, networking) using compromised credentials to map the environment before further exploitation.
defensiveControls: Implement least-privilege IAM -- no broad read permissions
Segment cloud accounts to limit discovery scope
Deploy real-time anomaly detection on API calls
Implement VPC endpoints to restrict API call sources
detectionMethods: Detect enumeration API call patterns (list-*, describe-*, get-*)
Alert on API calls from new user agents or SDKs
Monitor for rapid-fire API calls across multiple services
Detect enumeration from non-standard source IPs

mappings.techniqueName: Exfiltration Over Web Service: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
mappings.description: Adversary exfiltrates data to cloud storage services (attacker-owned S3 bucket, Dropbox, Google Drive, OneDrive) to blend with normal cloud traffic.
defensiveControls: Implement egress controls blocking access to unauthorized cloud storage endpoints
Deploy DLP with content inspection on egress
CASB to detect and block unauthorized cloud storage uploads
Restrict outbound access to approved cloud service domains
detectionMethods: Monitor DNS queries for cloud storage service domains
Detect large outbound data transfers to cloud storage APIs
Alert on egress traffic to unknown cloud storage endpoints
DLP alerts on sensitive data movement to external cloud services

mappings.description: Adversary disables or modifies cloud logging (CloudTrail, Azure Activity Log, GCP Audit Log) to prevent detection of subsequent malicious activity.
defensiveControls: Protect logging configuration with SCPs/policies -- prevent trail deletion or modification
Centralise logs in separate account with restricted access
Implement immutable log storage (Object Lock)
Alert immediately on any logging configuration change
detectionMethods: Real-time alerting on CloudTrail/audit log modification or deletion
Monitor for StopLogging, DeleteTrail, or equivalent API calls
Alert on log destination bucket policy changes
Secondary monitoring system as backup detection

mappings.description: Adversary uses compromised cloud resources for cryptocurrency mining (cryptojacking), sending spam, or launching attacks against other targets. Generates unexpected costs and indicates broader compromise.
defensiveControls: Implement billing anomaly detection with automated alerts
Restrict compute instance types and quantities through SCPs/quotas
Deploy CSP threat detection (GuardDuty, Defender, SCC)
Block egress to known mining pool IPs
detectionMethods: Billing anomaly detection (unexpected compute charges)
CSP threat detection alerts (GuardDuty CryptoCurrency finding)
High CPU utilisation alerts on non-compute-intensive workloads
Network flow analysis for connections to mining pools

mappings.description: Adversary encrypts cloud data (EBS volumes, S3 objects, databases) with attacker-controlled keys, rendering data inaccessible. Cloud-specific ransomware variant that leverages CSP encryption APIs.
defensiveControls: Implement immutable backups (Object Lock, vault lock) in separate account
Restrict KMS key creation/import permissions
Deploy deletion protection on critical databases
Implement versioning on cloud storage
detectionMethods: Alert on unusual KMS key creation or import
Monitor for mass encryption operations on storage
Detect unusual S3 PutObject with server-side encryption using non-standard keys
Alert on database snapshot deletion or modification
